review_title: Easy to set up and run
description: Although we are only a small company, we were trying to manually record our employees' holiday and sick leave as well as days in lieu, but found that we were losing track of vital information. CakeHR lets us keep all this information in an organised way, and lets our employees also keep track of their accrued allowances, as well as requesting leave in a more formal and organised manner.
author: Nicole S.
pros: I really liked the choice of price points, depending on the needs of your business. The user interface is also intuitive, and the calendar integrates well with Outlook.
cons: The trial version didn't have New Zealand public holidays automatically included, so I had to add these manually.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A Powerful HR Solution For Business
author: Paola O.
pros: This software makes handling our HR needs easy. It is well equipped and effortless to deploy and manage. It makes bettering our workforce easy.
cons: No serious flaws with Sage HR. It meets needs.

review_title: Works if your company is in a bind
author: Lacey H.
pros: I liked the announcement section and the company view to see the employees as you are able to see them by face and name and not just a list.
cons: It was hard to set up correctly and the customer service was awful and not very helpful and since I'm in the U.S and they are in the U.K they always seemed to be gone. For instance i needed some help at 1pm on a Thursday and the response message was we are closed but someone will get back to you Monday. Only through the chat bot on the screen, there is no number to call and if you do you get the "other" sage companies who transfer you around to everyone until you reach someone who says call this number and you end up calling Canada who says "umm you have the wrong number"

author: Melissa Daniela C.
pros: With Sage HR, we have best features and it makes HR processes easy. It is an affordable solution that is highly customizable to meet business needs.
cons: With Sage HR, no regrets whatsoever and it meets business set needs.

review_title: Appropriate for companies that want one system for whole HR/payroll process
description: Good experience so far. Does lack some out of the box functionalities but can be customized. But the team is having fun with the system so far.
author: Vershley V.
pros: Navigating through the system is very easy and does not necessarily require full training before the end user starts using it. The reports and dashboards are on point for higher management for decision making. And simple requirements can often be easily implemented by the administrator.
cons: If the system is not properly maintained, the data can quickly become irrelevant and reports are no longer accurate. The should be a team to maintain the system and make sure all data being entered by the employees are correct and regular. For small companies with only a few employees this can be a difficult task.

description: I use a very basic set of Sage HR functional, but it still makes me face some issues, which is not the most comfortable experience. Calendar and Company tabs are OK
author: Anna M.
pros: It has basic functional needed for a HRM process. The dev team adds new features and updates from time to time.
cons: The main and critical functional still has A LOT of issues. For example, we have a limited amount of Emergency Days available, BUT Sage do not show me any alerts when people overuse the Policy. Which makes me basically keep a track of things by myself. Also, almost a year ago I reported a few bugs to a customer support, and I still see those bugs from time to time. E.g. I don't think it's necessary to have a "death" item in a dropdown regarding the termination reason.... Like seriously? You guys already have "other" and I personally think it's enough

description: It satisfy our needs, but I would expect insights from the tool & a much better UX that is lacking.
author: Dina D.
pros: The application is salesforce based & the IT staff can easily configure change requests, new processes, reports, etc,...
cons: The UX is old-fashioned - the employees & managers do not like it.It is far from best of breed in capabilities & processes.

description: Great experience and excellent system for anyorganization to onboard.
author: Isaac M.
pros: Web based making it easily accessible across a wide variety of devices.
cons: It is generally OK but could end users make use of added functionality such as the ability of users to upload own achievments.

review_title: Sage HR: Revolutionizing Remote Management with Next-Level Competency Matching
description: Overall, Sage HR delivers on its promise of empowering businesses with modern HR solutions. It's hands-down a must-have for anyone who wants to manage their people smarter and more efficiently.
author: Happy C.
pros: There are many, but the easiest to point out is the remote people management accessibility as well as the competency marrying feature that is a mind-blowing feature.
cons: For someone who is not well familiar with Sage software, it would be slightly slow for them to catch up.

review_title: Finally a great tool for HR teams!
description: I've used Sage HR in a previous experience and I had an amazing time using it.
author: Mathieu L.
pros: There are many things to like about Sage HR: - First, the software is always updated with the latest legislation which is crucial and very helpful! - The platform is really intuitive (which is not always the case for HR tools) - The customer service is amazing
cons: - The company owns a lot of thing on the platform (including the settings) so it's better to have an expert or someone trained who can then be the point of contact for new joiners or users for example

description: Overall the system has been good, although there are some improvements that are needed to make it more user friendly
author: Daniel P.
pros: The simple but effective hr app makes it easy for employees and employers, with integrated training sections, payslips and p60s
cons: It isn’t as mobile friendly as advertised with some employees stating that payslips are difficult to read and the full payslip doesn’t show, also it doesn’t allow me to use integrated employee assistance app such as Wagestream, salary finance

review_title: My Outstanding experience with Sage HR.
author: Asnath G.
pros: I like how it's simple to hire new job candidates with Sage HR.It is simple to track employees time and attendance with Sage HR.It is a reliable tool for employees performance appraisal.It streamlines employees onboarding and recruiting process.
cons: I lack anything to highlight as drawback with Sage HR because it achieves all my objectives.

description: My experience was very positive. My interaction with Sage HR illustrated this software as an intuitive and comprehensive platform that streamlines HR processes, enhancing both administrative efficiency and employee engagement.
author: Daniel S.
pros: Sage HR, an HR management offer solution regarding remote employee tracking, engagement, and management, mirroring the ease of office-based interactions. Being modular it does not confuse user by too many options and users can only have services that they really needed without having "Noise services". Its integrated platform automates tedious HR tasks and provides employees with secure, instant access to necessary information through individual logins.
cons: Now it has about seven versatile modules, including Core HR, Leave Management, and Recruitment, Sage HR streamlines everything from onboarding to performance evaluation. Features like customizable workflows, comprehensive reports, and an intuitive dashboard empower businesses to manage the workforce efficiently while safeguarding sensitive data. However, having even more modules such as file management could be beneficial for certain teams such as teams of scientists.

author: Stefan W.
pros: For our company, it greatly simplifies the managing the holiday entitlement of our employees. Our employees appreciate the real-time view of their day-off and holiday balance.
cons: - Day Offs: In our company the day offs are flexible. Employees are supposed to take 2 day-offs per week, but sometimes these day-offs are shifted to the next week. (It's always within the same month though.) It would be great to have some way to be alerted/monitor if someone requests more day-offs than they are entitled to. Right now I have to count the day off requests to make sure it's within their day-off limit. (Right now a staff could request more day-offs that they are entitled to.) Having this said, I don't want it to be shown as a yearly quota, same holidays and public holidays, as these days can be taken anytime, while day-offs are not of that nature. However, a monthly quota would be fine. - Time Tracking: What is definitely missing is time tracking (for which we use HubStaff now) and overtime/undertime tracking. Our employees track their working time, and have a floating balance of time. Cake would be the complete and perfect HR solution if there was everything at a glance - the balance of Holiday, Public Holiday, Sick Days, Day-Offs and Over/Undertime. Ideally, being able to differentiate between freelancers and full-time staff - where freelancer time results in salary and full-time staff time results in time balance. Also, overtime should be able to be mutated (to holiday / day-off) or pay-off (define a per-hour amount for the time, calculate the pay-off, deduct the time from the balance.)

description: Really great experience overall, have zero complaints
author: Melissa L.
pros: The ease of use, easy to input information and navigate through the software, it took abit of getting use to but after that was perfect
cons: It takes some getting used to, but once you get the hang of it, nothing negative to say. I would just say spend time learning and playing through the system for easier faster use

review_title: The Best HR Features With Sage HR
author: Suresh G.
pros: One thing I like about Sage HR is that it offers top notch human resource features including performance management capabiliites. I like the time, attendance, and applicant tracking features It comes with employee scheduling and is also mobile friendly
cons: No regrets after using Sage HR for a long time now

review_title: One of the quickest growing HR systems on the market
description: Only second to one system, which is unaffordable for us. I look forward to seeing how this company develops. I recommend this system for any growing startup and SME.
author: Anna J.
pros: Very responsive team. Slack integration of the leave management is handy and updates in realtime Leave management is really good, I like that you can extra mandatory fields. We use this to track out of office staff, and remote working. It's also used to track time spent on their personal wellbeing. The app is handy, I know most of the staff use this instead of the web version. The org chart with collapsable levels and drag and drop changes is a very strong feature.
cons: most cons are just potential development and improvement as apposed to major flaws. Adding timesheets could be more streamlined, maybe a quick add from a calendar style overview would be nicer. Expense management - it would be great to be able to integrate with Xero.

author: Calum R.
pros: The annual leave section is very useful when managing a team.
cons: The system can sometimes be a bit clunky and not the most user friendly.

description: Could be a good choice, but functionality has to be improved
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: User friendly Flexible (at least in concept)
cons: No recruitment module Not enough fields in recipes (I am pretty sure that it is possible to let user to use much more variables and logic operators (jumps, triggers) and play with boolean search and complex logic). No calendar of upcoming appraisals for all the company, which could be useful for HR service to plan the load (I mean, standard calendar table view for the whole year) No recurrence (you should duplicate the same task manually, instead of setting of recurrence 'once per every 6/12/18 months etc") When you change initial task itself, the workflow which includes this task, still contains not updated version. As for me, if any component has been changed, it should be relevantly changed in any more complicated structures which contain that 'brick', instead of manual searching where this component could be used and further manual changing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Cake is great for small companies
description: Overall we are happy and will continue to use Cake for hopefully many more years. We hope our requestes will be taken to development
author: Anna R.
pros: It was very easy to setup it´s quiet user friendly it allows some automations vie receipts price is based on users
cons: as we grow very fast our needs change, even tough the cake team did implement a few changes to also accommodate our needs some are not satisfied yet: option to choose a worktime pattern when adding new user complex leave policy setup with formula for holiday allowance based on i.e. (age, number of children etc.)

description: I love the attitude and easy way of working with Cake HR! attitude, customer oriented mindset and ability to look on us as unique client , to listen to needs and respond ... My experience is really great! and even if there is missing some functionality - cake hr is seraching for the ways to improve it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Cake HR helps to fix the basics, to have a structure for people management matters. Excellent soft, have helped a lot in case where we needed to start from scratch and move from excel to something more up to date in HR .
cons: some functionality is missing what I have experienced with global providers. example - access rights, management between different companies if on holding.

description: The CakeHR, although with some issues, has quite the potential, especially if the developers continue improving and updating it. Not to mention that it is cheap in comparison to, for example, same BambooHR and it also fulfills pretty much all the needed options. I was pleasantly surprised with the customer support that I was provided while working with this software: if you have any questions or issues - the support team will always answer you within a day and try their best to adjust the software's settings to your liking and if you still have any further questions - they will arrange a meeting to help you solve the problem. The CakeHR is especially convenient for those companies that are functioning within one country. However, even if the company has several branches located in several countries it is still not a problem. Although it can be a bit confusing at first with all these access levels and settings that you will have to think about - with the right tags and teams you will manage it just fine.
author: Olga B.
pros: The Cake is very convenient for employees in terms of usage: it is simple, understandable, and solves the problem with the time-off policies' usage tracking.
cons: There were quite a few issues that weren't particularly to my liking; however, after mentioning them to the CakeHR support team - they were quickly fixed and changed in the way we wanted them to. Still, there is one thing that I think needs an improvement. As of now, when you want to upload the documents - you cannot filter/restrict them by location or the team. You can either upload it for everyone within the company to see it or upload it individually. As you might have already realized from that - if you want only one department or only one country's branch to see a certain document - you will have to upload it individually every time.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: This software suite will allow managment to automate internal human resource processes, that were previously completed manually. This has improved our internal efficiencies.
author: Shawn Y.
pros: What I liked about this software is the ease to automate internal processes.This software will allow a complete electronic profile to be created for all employees.
cons: I found there was a bit of a learning curve to become a fluent user. This software may be problematic for non-computer literate employees.

description: for two years of use and this software will always be within my reach. I love the flexibility of the software.
author: Manou E.
pros: this software is an incredible, efficient management of my staff, editing pay slips, remote management. I love this software.
cons: adding certain headings is complicated in my opinion.

description: Cake HR has been a huge help streamilining the PTO system at our preschool. We have around 17 teachers and the transparency of who is off and when they are off, makes my jobs easier. Teachers can see what dates are available and what are not, so I don't always have to be the bad guy!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I really like the calendar and the upcoming two weeks schedule. I am in charge of scheduling employees and I try to do the schedule in two week clips, so it is an easy way to see who is off. I also appreciate that keeps a record of people's use of PTO. So I can look back over the past year and see the dates and reasons people have taken off.
cons: It would be helpful to get reminders when people are close to having used all of their PTO, instead of having to through each employee and check. I would like to be able to set an automatic "roll over" to the next year, since we allow three days to roll over.
